tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: प्रधानमंत्री नरेंद्र मोदी की सुरक्षा में कटौती पर पूर्व रॉ प्रमुख सामंत गोयल की कड़ी चेतावनी: “ड्रोन, स्नाइपर और वैश्विक आतंकी खतरों के इस दौर में प्रधानमंत्री की सुरक्षा घटाना राष्ट्रहित में नहीं”, बोले— 25 वर्षों से मोदी आतंकी संगठनों के निशाने पर, सुरक्षा और मजबूत करने की जरूरत

Former R&AW Secretary Samant Goel has strongly opposed any reduction in Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s security convoy, warning that the current global security environment is more dangerous and unpredictable than ever before.

Goel emphasized that security is never a status symbol—it is determined solely by threat assessment. He noted that PM Modi has remained under threat for over 25 years, beginning from his tenure as Chief Minister of Gujarat, and that his firm stance on national security makes him a constant target for hostile elements, including jihadi groups and adversarial state-backed networks.

Citing multiple assassination attempts on US President Donald Trump despite America’s extensive security apparatus, Goel argued that India should strengthen, not downsize, the Prime Minister’s protection. He highlighted emerging threats such as armed drones, long-range sniper weapons, and lone-wolf attackers, all of which require enhanced and adaptive security protocols.

According to Goel, reducing the convoy could make it easier for adversaries to identify which vehicle carries the Prime Minister. He also pointed out that foreign governments often calibrate their own security arrangements for visiting leaders based on the standards maintained in their home countries.

His message was unequivocal: the Prime Minister’s security is not about optics or symbolism. It is a core national security priority that safeguards India’s leadership continuity, decision-making capability, and strategic stability.

When fighter jets enter MRO, paint is often stripped off to inspect structural deformation, fatigue points and microscopic surface cracks hidden beneath coatings.  

This is why you sometimes see aircraft flying or parked with peeled surfaces.  

Aerospace-grade epoxy fillers and repair compounds from companies like PPG Industries, Henkel and 3M are commonly used for aerodynamic surface restoration, these aren’t new frames.

“Israel just sent them [the emirates] Iron Dome batteries and personnel to help operate them,” he said during a talk at Tel Aviv University on 12 May 2026 to illustrate the potential benefits to Gulf Arab states of good relations with Israel.

Axios first reported the deployment of the Iron Dome systems to the UAE on 26 April, citing one US and two Israeli officials.
